title: paeonia mascula subsp. atlantica
intro: Paeonia mascula subsp. atlantica, also known as the Atlantic peony, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Paeonia, native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Atlantic coast of Europe. The roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and are believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Paeonia mascula subsp. atlantica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of conditions, from menstrual cramps to arthritis. The plant is also used in some traditional remedies to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
